checks.description: When res_valid transitions high, the 16-bit result must represent a mathematically correct division of the most recent dividend/divisor pair respecting the sign flag.
notes: The check does not assume a fixed latency; it only requires that a valid result appears after a legal request.
If multiple requests are in flight, the oracle conservatively matches the result to the latest request that has not yet been consumed.
checks.pass_condition: Whenever res_valid rises, compute expected_quotient and expected_remainder from the last captured dividend/divisor respecting sign. The upper 8 bits of result must equal expected_remainder and the lower 8 bits must equal expected_quotient. No X or Z values are permitted on result when res_valid is high.
checks.trigger_condition: On each rising edge of res_valid.

checks.description: After a single legal operation request (opn_valid high with res_valid low), the design must eventually produce a result that matches the division of the provided operands.
notes: The oracle does not enforce a specific number of cycles; it only requires eventual correctness once res_valid asserts.
checks.pass_condition: When res_valid becomes high following the request, the result must encode the correct quotient and remainder as defined in the contract. The check tolerates any latency between request and response.
checks.trigger_condition: On each occurrence of res_valid rising after a request.
